skill.name: tikzjax-diagramming
skill.description: Create precise TikZJax diagrams for Obsidian notes, including geometry, circuits, chemistry, plots, and technical illustrations.
skill.summary: Build precise TikZJax diagrams for Obsidian with LaTeX-style drawing guidance.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and path traversal patterns. Manual review found these are false positives caused by LaTeX and TikZ examples in documentation, with no executable scripts, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: LaTeX Commands Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings map to TikZ and LaTeX examples such as document wrappers, drawing commands, nodes, package loading, and diagram environments. These are Markdown examples for Obsidian TikZJax rendering, not Ruby backticks, shell substitution, or executable automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced TikZ or LaTeX documentation examples. No shell interpreter, script file, subprocess API, or user-controlled command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts match ordinary documentation words and diagram labels, including description text, table headings, style names, and grid examples. No cryptographic code, host inspection, process enumeration, or environment probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are prose, Markdown tables, or TikZ drawing examples. They do not contain APIs or instructions that perform cryptography or system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal Pattern in Placeholder Syntax
low_findings.description: The filesystem finding points to an ellipsis inside a TikZ environment description. It describes where drawing code belongs and does not reference filesystem paths or traversal sequences used for file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is Markdown documentation containing TikZ placeholder notation. There is no file read, file write, path join, or path normalization behavior to exploit.

content.value_statement: Technical notes often need diagrams that Mermaid cannot position precisely. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate TikZJax-ready diagrams for Obsidian.
actual_capabilities: Creates TikZJax code blocks with required document wrappers for Obsidian.
Provides examples for geometric shapes, coordinate systems, circuits, chemistry, plots, and commutative diagrams.
Explains supported TikZ packages and libraries used by TikZJax.
Guides dark mode friendly text and color choices for Obsidian rendering.
Helps choose TikZJax instead of Mermaid, Desmos, or MathJax for suitable diagrams.
limitations: Does not render or validate diagrams by itself.
Depends on the Obsidian TikZJax plugin and its supported package set.
Documents unsupported color mixing and Korean text limitations.
Generated diagrams may need manual adjustment for complex layouts.

use_cases.title: Precise Math Notes
use_cases.description: Create coordinate systems, geometry sketches, and labeled diagrams for lecture notes or study material.

use_cases.title: Engineering Reference Diagrams
use_cases.description: Draft circuit diagrams, 3D axes, and annotated technical figures inside Obsidian documentation.

use_cases.title: Research Knowledge Bases
use_cases.description: Add commutative diagrams, chemical structures, and plot sketches to structured research notes.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Shape Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian TikZJax diagram with a labeled rectangle, a highlighted area, and theme-friendly text.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Geometry Figure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TikZJax coordinate plane with axes, grid lines, three labeled points, and dashed guide lines.

prompt_templates.title: Circuit or Chemistry Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian TikZJax diagram using the correct package for a simple RC circuit or a small molecule.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Technical Illustration
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-part TikZJax technical figure with layers, annotations, a legend, and dark mode friendly labels.

output_examples.input: I need a precise coordinate diagram for a lesson on slopes.
output_examples.output: A TikZJax-ready diagram plan with axes, grid lines, labeled points, dashed rise and run guides, and readable labels.

output_examples.input: I need an Obsidian diagram for a basic resistor-capacitor circuit.
output_examples.output: A circuitikz-based diagram description with a resistor, capacitor, battery, closed loop, and clear component labels.

output_examples.input: I need a commutative diagram for a category theory note.
output_examples.output: A tikz-cd layout with four objects, directed arrows, labels, and guidance on arrow modifiers.

best_practices: Use the required document wrapper so TikZJax can render the diagram.
Keep scale near 1 unless the final note layout requires adjustment.
Omit explicit text colors when labels should adapt to Obsidian themes.
anti_patterns: Do not use TikZJax for simple flowcharts that Mermaid can express faster.
Do not rely on unsupported color mixing syntax in TikZJax diagrams.
Do not use custom colors when dark mode inversion must work automatically.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create TikZJax diagrams for Obsidian notes using TikZ and supported LaTeX packages.

faq.question: Does it render diagrams directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides diagram guidance and TikZJax-ready content for use with the Obsidian plugin.

faq.question: When should I use TikZJax instead of Mermaid?
faq.answer: Use TikZJax when precise coordinates, geometry, circuits, chemistry, or advanced technical drawings are needed.

faq.question: Can it make diagrams that work in dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends theme-friendly label choices and explains TikZJax black and white inversion behavior.

faq.question: Which packages are documented?
faq.answer: The skill covers tikz-cd, circuitikz, pgfplots, chemfig, tikz-3dplot, and common math packages.

faq.question: Are there known TikZJax limits?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation notes limits for color mixing, custom color inversion, Korean text, and very small scales.
